insights:
The single best deal in the market right now is DeepSeek’s DeepSeek V4 Flash. At a blended price of $0.06 per million tokens, it delivers an intelligence score of 40 out of 60, a speed of 99 tokens per second, and a 1000K context window. Its composite value score of 666.7 is more than double that of any model costing above $0.06 per million tokens, making it the clear winner on price-to-performance for general-purpose use. For most production workloads that require decent reasoning without blowing the budget, this model offers the highest quality per dollar spent.

Two notable runners-up deserve attention, each with specific trade-offs. MiMo-V2.5, also priced at $0.06 per million tokens, has a slightly lower intelligence score of 37 but a strong arena code Elo of 1429, making it a better choice if coding ability is a priority. It runs at 80 t/s with the same 1000K context. For those willing to pay more for top-tier intelligence, MiniMax M3 costs $0.22 per million tokens and scores 44 intelligence with a 1496 code Elo, but its value score falls to 200.0. InclusionAI’s Ling-2.6-flash at the same $0.06 price is extremely fast at 166 t/s but only manages 14 intelligence, limiting it to very simple tasks.

For the most common use case—cost-sensitive API calls where you want the best possible reasoning without a high per-query bill—the recommendation is clear: use DeepSeek V4 Flash as your default. It balances intelligence, speed, and cost better than any other model listed. If a slight increase in reasoning quality is critical for your application and you can accept 80 t/s, MiMo-V2.5 is an equally priced alternative with stronger coding benchmarks. Avoid paying more than $0.06 per million tokens unless your task demonstrably requires the extra intelligence of MiniMax M3 or DeepSeek V4 Pro.
